brief_document.title: Embedded-replica sync against a remote primary
brief_document.purpose: Prove the distributed deployment path: a local embedded replica that syncs to a remote libSQL primary. This is the one part of the crate that needs an external service, so its test is runtime env-gated rather than ignored, per CLAUDE.md.
brief_document.task: Add an embedded-replica sync integration test that, when a remote primary is configured via AION_LIBSQL_TEST_URL (and token), opens a LibSqlStore in EmbeddedReplica mode, appends and syncs, and asserts the data reaches the primary / is readable through a second replica after sync. When the env var is unset, the test emits a tracing skip line and returns Ok trivially. It is never #[ignore]. Confirm the same EventStore behaviour holds in replica mode as in embedded mode.

requirements.title: Env-gated embedded-replica sync test
requirements.spec: WHILE AION_LIBSQL_TEST_URL is set, THE SYSTEM SHALL run the embedded-replica sync test against that primary; WHILE it is unset, THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a tracing skip line and return Ok without failing. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT mark the test #[ignore].
acceptance: tests/replica_sync.rs reads AION_LIBSQL_TEST_URL (and the accompanying auth token env var, e.g. AION_LIBSQL_TEST_TOKEN) at runtime.
When unset, the test emits a tracing::info! skip line naming the missing env var and returns Ok(()) — it does not fail and is not #[ignore]d.
When set, the test constructs a LibSqlConfig::EmbeddedReplica pointing at the primary with the token and opens a LibSqlStore.
The gate is a runtime read of the env var, not a cfg!/feature/#[ignore] compile-time gate.

requirements.title: Sync round-trip assertion
requirements.spec: WHEN the env-gated test runs against a real primary, THE SYSTEM SHALL append history through the embedded replica, sync to the primary, and observe the synced data so that the distributed path is proven end-to-end.
acceptance: The test appends a known event batch through the replica store and triggers/awaits a sync to the primary.

The test asserts the data is durably present on the primary — either by reading it back through a second freshly-opened replica that syncs from the primary, or by querying the primary directly — and asserts read_history matches what was appended.
The same EventStore contract behaviour (append ordering, read_history) holds in replica mode as in embedded mode; the test reuses the contract assertions rather than asserting libSQL internals.
Sync or connection failures surface as StoreError::Backend, not panics.

boundaries: SHALL NOT require a live primary for the crate to build or for the rest of the test suite to pass — only this test consults the primary, and only when its env var is set.
SHALL NOT use #[ignore] — the gate is a runtime env-var read per CLAUDE.md.
SHALL NOT re-implement the EventStore methods — replica mode reuses the same impl; only the connection-open path differs (AS-002 R2).
SHALL NOT add distributed shard-assignment or coordination logic — that is engine/distributed scope, not this store.
verification: cargo test -p aion-store-libsql passes with AION_LIBSQL_TEST_URL unset (the replica test skips with a tracing line, all other tests run).
With AION_LIBSQL_TEST_URL pointing at a real primary, cargo test -p aion-store-libsql runs the replica sync test and it passes.
A reviewer confirms the test uses a runtime env-gate (not #[ignore], not a feature flag) and emits a visible skip line when unset.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: Embedded-replica sync is exercised by a test runtime-gated on AION_LIBSQL_TEST_URL: when the env var is unset the test emits a tracing skip line and returns Ok; it is never marked #[ignore].

stories.text: As an engine operator, I want the same store crate to drive a distributed deployment via an embedded replica syncing to a remote primary, so that I do not have to learn or wire a second backend to scale out.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses per CLAUDE.md. Tests needing a remote primary use a runtime env-gate, not #[ignore].
lib.rs is declarations and re-exports only; logic lives in named modules.
500-line file limit (excluding tests/comments/whitespace). The EventStore impl is split across append, read, and timer modules.
Depends only on aion-store (and transitively aion-core) among Aion crates — no dependency on the engine, beamr, or any other backend. Structural; must hold.
Raw SQL is permitted here because this crate IS a storage backend (the storage layer). No other crate issues SQL against this store; they go through the EventStore trait.
WorkflowStatus is derived from event history via aion-core's projection. No mutable stored status field; a cached projection must never diverge from the events.
LibSqlStore must pass the shared AC-007 conformance suite unmodified — the same behavioural standard as the oracle.
append's sequence guard and event inserts execute in a single libSQL transaction; a mismatch writes nothing and returns SequenceConflict — no partial batch is ever persisted.
LibSqlConfig is Deserialize-only and carries no hardcoded durability/WAL defaults; tunable values are surfaced for the operator to set, per CLAUDE.md no-assumed-defaults.
All libsql and serde errors are mapped into the StoreError taxonomy at the crate boundary; no foreign error type leaks across the EventStore surface.

resolved_context.intention: When an engine operator embeds Aion and writes no storage configuration, this is the store they get: a single file on disk, zero external infrastructure, full durability. The same crate serves the distributed path — the local file becomes an embedded replica syncing to a remote primary — without the operator learning a second backend. The in-memory reference store (cluster AC) proves the contract but forgets on restart; LibSqlStore makes durability the easy default, held to the identical behavioural standard as the oracle. A workflow that sleeps for months and resumes needs its history to survive every crash and reboot in between. When this cluster is done, an operator writes LibSqlStore::open("app.db") and has a production-grade durable event store the engine cannot tell apart from the oracle except that it remembers; the shared AC-007 conformance suite passes against it unchanged, plus a durability-specific suite proving state survives close-and-reopen.
